mnemonics: Every Product Eventually Ends (EPEE): Extraction, Production, Energy Use, End-of-Life - the four key stages of the product life cycle in Green IT.
keyPoints: The product life cycle includes raw material extraction, manufacturing, distribution, usage, and disposal/recycling.
Designing for energy efficiency during the usage phase is a critical focus area for reducing a product's overall environmental impact.
Sustainable end-of-life management, including recycling and proper e-waste handling, is essential to close the resource loop and prevent pollution.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on energy consumption during the use phase and ignoring the significant environmental impacts of manufacturing and disposal.
Assuming that digital products and services (e.g., cloud storage) are carbon-neutral, overlooking the energy-intensive data centers that support them.
